page: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the m8a.4xlarge demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, Geekbench single-core scores, and single-threaded gzip compression. Multi-core performance is average, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling in the middle 50%. Memory performance is strong, with the PassMark memory score in the top 25%, though uncached memory bandwidth read and write speeds remain in the average tier. For specialized workloads, the server achieves top-tier results in image and text processing, as well as text generation for small language models.

Given its dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 memory, the m8a.4xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a cost-efficient option for compute-intensive tasks that rely on network-attached storage. It is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, text and image processing, and running small-scale language model inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps and contains no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and text processing, alongside strong memory performance and static web serving throughput. Multi-core compute and database operations perform in the average tier. This configuration is highly suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, software compilation, and small-scale language model inference.
